compiled_state.title: As a developer, my commits are blocked if they introduce hardcoded home-directory paths
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a check-portability.sh pre-commit lint and fix all existing hardcoded paths in tracked files.
**Why**: Hardcoded absolute paths are the root cause of the portability failures. A pre-commit guard prevents regression, and fixing existing paths ensures a clean baseline.
**Scope**:
- IN: Create check-portability.sh scanning staged files for /Users/<name>/ and /home/<name>/ patterns; add # portability-ok inline suppression; register as pre-commit hook; fix hardcoded paths in CLAUDE.md, .claude/dso-config.conf, .dso-plugin-path, plugins/dso/docs/WORKTREE-GUIDE.md (with suppression for doc examples); ensure test-plugin-scripts.sh passes with zero matches
- OUT: Modifying check-shim-refs.sh or any other pre-commit hook from the Enforce DSO shim usage epic — these are distinct hooks

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a pre-commit hook runs check-portability.sh on staged files and blocks commits containing absolute home-directory paths (/Users/<name>/ or /home/<name>/)
  ← Satisfies: "A pre-commit lint check (check-portability.sh) blocks commits that introduce absolute home-directory paths"
- When this story is complete, documentation files can use # portability-ok inline suppression to exempt intentional example paths from the check
  ← Satisfies: "Supports # portability-ok inline suppression for documentation examples"
- When this story is complete, the existing test in test-plugin-scripts.sh passes with zero matches for hardcoded paths across all plugin scripts
  ← Satisfies: "The existing test in test-plugin-scripts.sh that greps for hardcoded paths passes independently with zero matches"
- When this story is complete, all previously hardcoded paths in tracked files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/dso-config.conf, .dso-plugin-path, WORKTREE-GUIDE.md) are either removed, made dynamic, or annotated with # portability-ok
  ← Satisfies: SC1 prerequisite (hardcoded paths must be fixed for self-detection to work)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for check-portability.sh including suppression logic and both macOS/Linux path patterns
  ← Satisfies: unit test DoD requirement

## Considerations

- [Testing] Regex must cover both /Users/ (macOS) and /home/ (Linux) patterns
- [Scenario] check-portability.sh supports # portability-ok inline suppression comment for documentation examples

comments.body: Consideration: [Correctness] check-portability.sh must exempt dso-config.conf's dso.plugin_root value — this key inherently contains absolute paths. Either exclude dso-config.conf from the lint or handle dso.plugin_root as an expected exception.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
